---
title: rmarkdown + knitr
---

```{css css_custom, code=readLines("_css_custom.css"), echo=FALSE}
```
```{r knitr_config, code=readLines("_knitr_config.R"), echo=FALSE}
```

*********************************************************************

rmarkdown:  

* https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/  
* https://dr-harper.github.io/rmarkdown-cookbook/  
* https://www.markdownguide.org/basic-syntax/  
* https://rmarkdown.rstudio.com/lesson-1.html  
* https://www.rstudio.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/03/rmarkdown-reference.pdf  
* https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/  

knitr:  

* https://yihui.name/knitr/  
* https://yihui.name/knitr/demo/graphics/  
* https://kbroman.org/knitr_knutshell/  

misc:  

* https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/kableExtra/vignettes/awesome_table_in_html.html  

*********************************************************************

## images

`![](path/to/smallorb.png)` (markdown)

`knitr::include_graphics(img1_path_or_url)`

(HTML)

*TODO*

*********************************************************************

## links

### urls

```markdown
[linked phrase](http://example.com)
```

### document sections 

```markdown
# Missingness {#missingness}

blah blah

(see [Missingness](#missingness) section)
```

### tables and figures

* can't currently be done using `output: html_document`
* only available for bookdown documents

*********************************************************************

## verbatim chunks

* wrap the code in a chunk using 4 back ticks instead of three  

`````markdown
````
fancy markdown code
````
`````

* or wrap the code in `pre` and `code` tags 

```markdown
<pre><code>
fancy markdown code
</code></pre>
```

* in both cases, add the following text to the rhs of the closing curly brace at the start of any R (or other code) chunks in the fancy markdown code

```{r, echo=FALSE, comment=""}
cat("`r ''`")
```

*********************************************************************

## tabbed sections {.tabset}

```markdown
## tabbed section {.tabset}

### tab 1

some stuff

### tab 2

some more stuff
```

### tab 1

stuff

*********************************************************************

### tab 2

more stuff

*********************************************************************

## css styling

### document-level

#### yaml

```markdown
output:
  html_document:
    css: style.css
```

#### css chunk

These can be placed anywhere in the Rmd file, including above the yaml header.

````markdown
```{css, eval=FALSE}`r ''`
# make all chunk output wider
pre {
  max-height: 300px;
  float: left;
  width: 500px;
  overflow-y: auto;
}

pre.r {
  max-height: none;
}
```
````

### chunk-level

#### out.extra

````markdown
```{r, out.extra='style="background-color: #9ecff7; padding:10px; display: inline-block;'}`r ''`
code
```
````

#### class.ouput

* http://zevross.com/blog/2017/06/19/tips-and-tricks-for-working-with-images-and-figures-in-r-markdown-documents/

````markdown
```{css}`r ''`
# horizontal scroll chunk output
.scroll {
  max-height: 300px;
  float: left;
  width: 910px;
  overflow-y: auto;
}
```
````

````markdown
```{r, class.output = "scroll"}`r ''`
print(mtcars)
```
````

### selectors

#### code

selector | description
------------- | -------------
`pre` | preformatted text
`pre.r` | preformatted text with class "r" 
`code` | 

#### headings

selector | description
------------- | -------------
`#header` | title, subtitle, author and date (collectively)
`.title` | title
`.subtitle` | subtitle
`.author` | author
`.date` | date

#### tables

selector | description
------------- | -------------
`table` | 
`table caption` | 
`th` | table header
`tr` | table row
`td` | table cell

#### toc

selector | description
------------- | -------------
`#TOC` | table of contents
`#toc_container` | 
`.tocify` | toc class when using floating toc
`.toc_title` | 
`.toc-content` | body when using floating toc

*********************************************************************

## themes

available themes include:

* cosmo

the relevant css files can be found here:

```{r}
dir(system.file(package = "rmarkdown", "rmd/h/bootstrap/css"))
```


*********************************************************************

## highlight

*********************************************************************

## DT::datatable

*TODO*

*********************************************************************

## reusing code

### ref.label

E.g. evaluate a code chunk early on in the document but don't echo it, and then echo the same chunk later on but without evaluating it.

````markdown
```{r chunk1, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE}`r ''`
some fancy code
```

blah blah ...

```{r, ref.label="chunk1", eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}`r ''`
```
````

E.g. put all code in appendices (not convinced this works...)

````markdown
# Appendix: R code {-}

```{r ref.label=knitr::all_labels(engine="R"), echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE}`r ''`
```

# Appendix: python code {-}

```{python ref.label=knitr::all_labels(engine="python"), echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE}`r ''`
```
````

### code

E.g. echo an external R script without evaluating it.

````markdown
```{r, code=readLines("myscript.R", warn=FALSE), echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE}`r ''`
```
````

E.g. eval an external CSS script without echoing it.

````markdown
```{css, code=readLines("style.css", warn=FALSE), eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE}`r ''`
```
````

### child

* https://stackoverflow.com/questions/25824795/how-to-combine-two-rmarkdown-rmd-files-into-a-single-output

````markdown
---  
title: My Report  
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: yes 
---

```{r child = 'chapter1.Rmd'}`r ''`
```

```{r child = 'chapter2.Rmd'}`r ''`
```
````

*chapter1.Rmd*

```markdown
# Chapter 1

This is chapter 1.
```

*chapter2.Rmd*

```markdown
# Chapter 2

This is chapter 2.
```

*********************************************************************

## Appendix: example configuration chunk {-}

> It is recommended to create a knit configuration chunk as the first chunk in a script with cache = FALSE and include = FALSE options set. 

```{r knitr_config, code=readLines("_knitr_config.R"), echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE}
```

*********************************************************************

```css
body .main-container { 
  max-width: 1280px !important; 
  width: 1280px !important; 
} 
body { 
  max-width: 1280px !important;
}

img { width: 400px; display: block; margin: 0 auto; }
```

* https://jules32.github.io/rmarkdown-website-tutorial/getting_sophisticated.html

* https://francoisbirgand.github.io/tutorial-RMarkdown_instructions.html

* https://www.emilyzabor.com/tutorials/rmarkdown_websites_tutorial.html

* https://www.lucymcgowan.com
